new_interventions: Electronic component (IC chip) placed on white paper surface
Eggshell fragments used as soil amendment in P3/P4 area

inventory_reconciliation.notes: Registry P1 and P2 are currently devoid of visible vegetative growth. P3 contains a single leaf. P4 is indistinguishable from the P3/P4 shared soil mass.

plant_audit.p1: Bare soil, no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.p2: Bare soil, no signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.p3: Single leaf remaining, appears chlorotic/pale, structural integrity is low.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Damp, dark, appears to have high organic content.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3/P4 pot. Electronic component (IC chip) introduced on the desk surface.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t5: Initial state shows single leaf in P3.
temporal_deltas.t5_to_t3: Electronic component appears on the white paper.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_current: Electronic component remains; plant in P3 shows no significant growth or further decline, suggesting a state of stasis.

visual_health_inference.p3_pothos: Critical. The single leaf indicates a struggle for survival; lack of secondary growth suggests root system failure or extreme dormancy.

anomalies: Presence of an integrated circuit (IC) on the desk surface.
Eggshell fragments in soil (likely calcium supplementation attempt).
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of severe decline. P1 and P2 are effectively empty. P3 retains a single, fragile leaf. The introduction of an electronic component and eggshells suggests an experimental or non-traditional approach to plant care, which has not yet yielded positive growth results. The environment appears stable but unproductive.
